title: Blueberry sour cream cake
subtitle: A tender single-layer cake with suspended blueberries and a lemon crumb
excerpt: A straightforward American single-layer cake: a soured-cream batter that bakes to a tender, close crumb, filled with whole blueberries suspended throughout. The lemon zest cuts through the richness. Served in squares, not slices.
sourceNotes: Soured-cream cakes with fresh blueberries are a well-documented American home baking tradition from the mid-twentieth century onward, appearing widely in community cookbooks and home economics publications.

recipe.yieldDescription: One 23 cm square cake (12 to 16 squares)
recipe.freezeNotes: Cut into squares, wrap individually, and freeze. Thaw at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps at room temperature for 2 to 3 days, covered. Best on the day of baking.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan: 160 degrees C; check at 40 minutes

glossaryTerms.definition: Fresh blueberries tossed in a tablespoon of flour before folding into a batter tend to stay suspended rather than sinking to the bottom during baking. The coating slightly raises the density of each berry relative to the surrounding batter and creates a barrier that slows the berry sinking while the batter is still liquid and setting. It is not a guarantee, but it makes a measurable difference in cakes with a liquid batter.

content.text: This is a simple cake: one bowl for the dry ingredients, one for the wet, combined and baked in a square tin. The soured cream makes the crumb tender and gives the cake enough body to hold the blueberries in place. Toss the berries in a tablespoon of the measured flour before folding them in;
helps them stay suspended in the batter rather than collecting on the base. The lemon zest is not optional; without it the cake can taste flat.

Preheat the oven to 180 degrees C (conventional). Grease and line a 23 cm square tin. Whisk together all but one tablespoon of the flour with the baking powder, bicarbonate of soda, and salt in a bowl. Beat the butter and sugar together until pale and fluffy, about 3 minutes. Add the eggs one at a time, then the vanilla and lemon zest. Fold in the flour mixture alternately with the soured cream in three additions of flour and two of soured cream. Toss the blueberries in the reserved tablespoon of flour and fold gently into the batter. Tip into the prepared tin and spread level. Bake for 40 to 45 minutes, until a skewer comes out clean and the top is golden. Cool in the tin for 20 minutes before cutting into squares.
